text.title: graph-rag-retrieval Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for graph-rag-retrieval as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using community summaries as the answer for ENTITY or RELATIONSHIP queries instead of routing to the appropriate graph traversal path.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Community summaries are coarse-grained and mix multiple entities; they answer global thematic questions but blur the specific entity details and relationship paths that ENTITY and RELATIONSHIP queries need. Route by query type, not by what is cheapest to retrieve.

failure-mode.description: Running live graph traversal for GLOBAL queries at query time on graphs larger than 50k nodes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Full-graph traversal to assemble a global answer can take tens of seconds on large graphs. Pre-computed hierarchical summaries serve GLOBAL queries in milliseconds. The entire point of offline summarization in graph-rag-indexing is to make GLOBAL queries fast at retrieval time.
